chinese: adj. 感到滿意的 (happy for what has happened as one wishes)
example: Andy is very satisfied with the hamburger he made.

chinese: vt. 使滿意；使滿足
example: My report did not satisfy the teacher, so I was asked to rewrite it.

chinese: n. [C][U] 滿意；滿足
example: Vera got great satisfaction from teaching young children how to dance.

chinese: vt.; vi. 批評 (to say someone or something is bad or wrong)
example: The restaurant was strongly criticized for its terrible food.

chinese: n. [U][C] 批評
example: The man accepted the criticism of his poor performance at work.

chinese: n. [C][U] 悲劇；災難 (a very sad event, especially one involving suffering or death)
example: While the ship was sailing across the ocean, a tragedy occurred. All of the sailors died in an accident.

chinese: adj. 悲慘的
example: Upon learning the tragic news of Nathan's death, his parents were too shocked to accept the truth.

chinese: vt. 使生氣；使心煩 (to make someone angry, worried, or unhappy)
adj. 生氣的；心煩的
example: It upset Mark that his girlfriend was late for their date again.
Teresa was very upset that her brother entered her room without knocking on the door first.

chinese: adv. 非常；極度地 (very great in degree)
example: Vincent van Gogh's paintings are extremely valuable because he is one of the most influential artists in history.

chinese: adj. 尷尬的 (ashamed or uncomfortable about what other people will think of you)
example: Amber felt embarrassed when she fell down the stairs in front of the crowd.

chinese: vt. 使尷尬
example: It embarrassed Bob to hear his friends sing Happy Birthday to him in public.

chinese: vt. 刪除 (to remove the written words or information stored in a computer)
example: Lawrence was shocked after he deleted the important files by accident.

chinese: n. [C] 社群；社區 (a group of people who share the same things or live in the same area)
example: The whole community came together to help the family after their house caught fire.

chinese: adj. 殘忍的 (causing pain or suffering to others without feeling sorry)
example: It was cruel to leave the dog outside in the cold.

chinese: vt. 得罪；冒犯 (to make someone unhappy or angry)
example: The businessman was offended by the reporter's questions and left in anger.

chinese: n. [U] 冒犯
example: Please do not be angry about what I just said. I meant no offense.

chinese: adj. 冒犯的
example: Richard made an offensive joke about the blind musician in the park and upset many people.

chinese: n. [C][U] 謠言 (information spread from person to person that may or may not be true)
example: The rumor about the breakup of that celebrity couple spread quickly, but it turned out to be fake news.
notes: hear a rumor that + S + V: 聽說某人...
Rumor has it that + S + V: 據傳聞...
spread a rumor about: 散布關於...的謠言

chinese: n. [C][U] 懲罰 (an act of making someone suffer for doing something wrong)
example: Tim was not allowed to go out with friends for a month as a punishment for lying to his parents.

chinese: vt. 處罰
example: The teacher punished Sophia for cheating on the exam by giving her some extra homework.

chinese: n. [C][U] 身分 (who a person is)
example: The organization did not reveal the identity of the person who had donated a million dollars to the poor.
notes: identical (adj.): 完全相同的；極為相似的

chinese: n. [C] 目標對象 (someone or something that people aim at when attacking)
n. [C] 目標 (something that one is trying to get or achieve)
example: The thieves marked a red cross on the door of their target's house.
Dennis has set a target of buying his own car before he turns 30. That's why he works very hard.

chinese: n. [C] 受害者；犧牲者 (someone who has been hurt or killed)
example: Polar bears and penguins have become the victims of climate change. We need to come up with some solutions to save them.
notes: fall victim to sth./sb.: 成為...的受害者
murder victim: 謀殺案的受害者

chinese: vi. 交涉；交手 (to get involved with someone or begin fighting)
vi. 投入；從事 (to do or to get involved in an activity)
example: Isabel likes to engage with kids and wants to become a kindergarten teacher in the future.
Richard engages in several environmental activities in his free time.
notes: engage with sb.: 與某人交涉／交手

chinese: vt. 展現；顯示 (to clearly show something through proofs or examples)
example: Ken's gesture demonstrated his confusion about the right direction to the MRT.
notes: demonstrate against + N: 示威反對...

chinese: vt. 影響 (to make a change in someone or something)
example: The typhoon affected the prices of vegetables greatly this year.

chinese: adj. 刻薄的
n. [C] 美髮師
n. [C] 蟾蜍
adj. 嚴厲的；刺耳的
n. [U] 網路霸凌
n. [C] (在網路上) 故意留下激怒他人言論的人
n. [U] 惡意
n. [C] 喜劇演員
n. [C] 喜劇
n. [C] 長襪
n. [C] 網路公民
n. [C] 情商；情感商數
phr. 取笑；嘲弄 (to laugh at someone or something in an unkind way)
example: Some students [made fun of] Louis because his new glasses looked weird.

chinese: phr. 充滿 (to make something full)
example: At the wedding, the man said he would [fill] his wife's life [with] happiness for the rest of their lives.

chinese: phr. 遠離 (to avoid going near someone or something)
example: The firefighters asked the family to [keep away] from the burning house, or they might get hurt.

chinese: phr. 向……求助 (to get help or advice from someone or something)
example: The tourist did not know how to get to the museum, so he [turned to] the police for help.

chinese: phr. 樹立榜樣 (to behave in a way that other people should copy)
example: The boss always arrives at work on time in order to [set a] good [example] for the staff.

chinese: phr. 攻擊；向……衝去 (to move toward someone in order to attack)
example: The woman ran away quickly as the man suddenly [came at] her with a knife.
